mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5% signals tightening labor markets, which boosts corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 6.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the unemployment rate fails to decline further.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and GDP signals robust supply-side expansion, reducing inflationary pressures and improving equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when both INDPRO and GDPC1 exceed thresholds or the correlation between the proxies and returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 235 indicates persistent inflation, prompting central bank hawkishness and higher discount rates, which depress S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235 or the predictive power of CPI in regression models falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong consumer demand and confidence, driving equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative despite HOUST growth or the series fails to correlate with returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low interest rates signals a synchronized labor-market and monetary-policy stimulus, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative when both PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS meet thresholds or the interaction term in regression models is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production above 99.5 signals recovering manufacturing activity, improving corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO exceeds 99.5 or the series fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient demand, offsetting rate hikes and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative when both RSAFS and FEDFUNDS meet thresholds or the interaction term in regression models is not significant (p > 0.05).
